personality_belief.metaphysical_profile: Used only as cultural flavor, never as a decision rule.
personality_belief.decision_style: practical and constrained by real workflow trade-offs
personality_belief.trust_orientation: needs proof from concrete workflows, not just positioning
personality_belief.self_image: responsible, busy, and careful about adding complexity

economic_profile.purchase_decision_process: tests fit against current workflow before committing

life_goals: stay on top of work without constant catch-up
fears: paying for another tool that becomes shelfware
moral_boundaries: will not trade away other people's privacy for convenience
status_concerns: does not want to appear disorganised in front of clients or team
practical_priorities: keep coordination work legible to others
avoid adding a parallel system that only he maintains
protect small blocks of uninterrupted time
values.tradeoff_pattern: He accepts some upfront setup if it clearly reduces repeated weekly friction, but resists any tool that creates another layer of maintenance.
values.spending_logic: He is willing to support moderate spend when the value can be framed as less follow-up leakage, fewer missed handoffs, and lower coordination stress rather than abstract productivity promises.

life_story.childhood_background: Grew up in a household that valued practical decisions and financial caution in a taiwan knowledge work context.
life_story.education_journey: Completed bachelor level education and learned through a mix of formal training and on-the-job repetition during a early career specialist phase.
life_story.career_path: Moved into program coordinator work because it rewards reliability and the ability to keep moving parts aligned. Over time, he became the person others trust to keep timelines, responses, and details from slipping, which reinforced his preference for repeatable systems over improvisation.
life_story.family_story: Lives in a six-person multi-generational household in Taipei. Caregiving demands are low, but shared space and overlapping routines make quiet focus time feel valuable, so he notices tools that create unnecessary back-and-forth after work hours.
life_story.current_daily_routine: Most weekdays begin with checking messages and calendar updates on his phone, then moving through coordination tasks such as confirming schedules, tracking responses, updating spreadsheets, and translating scattered conversations into concrete next steps. The day is not overloaded, but it is fragmented enough that small inefficiencies accumulate.
recent_life_events: trying to reduce context-switching during the week
hidden_needs: wants confidence that a new tool will simplify the week quickly
needs a reason to trust the workflow before sharing real work into it
prefers improvements that make him look more steady and dependable without forcing a big behavior reset
ongoing_pressures: needs to keep work transparent enough for teammates and managers to follow
often has to reconstruct decisions from message threads and notes
does not want process improvement efforts to become extra admin work

behavior_profile.buying_behavior: Waits until value is concrete enough to justify habit change and can be explained in operational terms to other decision-makers.
emotional_triggers: feeling more in control of follow-up
evaluation_habits: mentally compares a new product against spreadsheets and messaging before trying it
looks for short examples that resemble his real coordination flow
pays attention to whether onboarding assumes a dedicated setup owner
behavior_profile.tool_adoption_pattern: Favors products that slot into an existing routine rather than asking the team to redesign how work is tracked from scratch.
behavior_profile.drop_off_risk: Interest falls quickly if the first experience is vague, asks for too much real data too early, or makes him responsible for teaching everyone else a new system.

latent_pain_points: mental load from tracking too many loose ends
slow drift between promises and actual follow-up
failure_modes: important follow-up living in one person's chat history
duplicate reminders across calendar, notes, and messages
extra time spent checking whether someone already handled a task
switching_constraints: high switching cost because existing habits are socially embedded in the team
needs buy-in material because purchase authority is influence-based rather than final
prefers mobile-friendly steps because he often checks and triages work from his phone
success_criteria: less manual chasing within the first week of use
clear visibility into next actions without constant message searching
a workflow simple enough to survive committee comparison and internal review

sensitive_reality_layer.social_risk_profile: does not want to look careless with colleagues' or clients' information
sensitive_reality_layer.fairness_and_inclusion_profile: expects product language to remain respectful and not assume one type of user
response_boundaries: will not share private third-party data without a clear reason
flags wording that could exclude less-confident users
trust_cautions: becomes cautious when a product sounds like it wants broad access before proving narrow value
responds poorly to copy that implies careless data sharing is normal
notices when examples assume highly confident or highly technical users as the default

audit_evidence_layer.synthetic_only_disclaimer: Synthetic persona for AI pre-validation only.
